sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: One paragraph: purpose of this procedure — to control QMS documents per ISO 13485:2016 clause 4.2.4 so the correct version is available at points of use and obsolete documents are prevented from unintended use.

sections.title: 2. Scope
sections.section_prompt: Specify which document types are controlled (procedures, work instructions, forms, templates, drawings, software DHF/SBOM lists). Distinguish controlled documents from records (records are governed by {ref:QP-004}).

rows.responsibility: Drafts new or revised documents using the controlled template; performs self-review.
Verifies technical accuracy and clause coverage.
Reviews for QMS compliance, ensures revision numbering and metadata are correct, manages the master list.
Approves release; signature is captured in the document approval block.
Initiates periodic review and change requests.

sections.title: 4. Procedure
sections.section_prompt: For EACH lettered sub-requirement of 4.2.4 (review and approve before issue; review, update and re-approve; identify changes and current revision; make relevant versions available at points of use; remain legible and identifiable; control external documents; prevent unintended use of obsolete documents), write the concrete steps THIS company takes, including the system/tool used (e.g. Google Drive controlled folder, Greenlight Guru, MasterControl, Jira). Include numbering scheme (e.g. QP-### Rev X.Y), change request workflow, and retention of obsolete copies marked OBSOLETE.

sections.title: 5. Revision Numbering and Change Classification
sections.section_prompt: Define major vs minor revision numbering (e.g. X.0 for substantive change requiring re-approval; X.Y for editorial). Define what triggers a major change (regulatory impact, risk re-assessment, process change). Define change classification: editorial / clarifying / substantive / regulatory.

sections.title: 6. Control of External Documents
sections.section_prompt: Describe handling of external documents (standards, regulations, supplier specifications): how they are identified, registered in {ref:QP-006}, kept current via subscription/alert, and distributed.

rows.description: Draft / In Review / Released / Superseded / Obsolete.
